children.name: writes category_groups (not groups) into the cached snapshot
cached snapshot is structurally identical to GET /api/catalog body
fetchCatalog derives If-None-Match from cached catalog_version
expense is in IndexedDB before network call starts
expense removed from queue only after 200
expense stays in queue on server 502
expense stays in queue on server 500
expense stays in queue on server 503
expense stays in queue on network timeout
expense stays in queue on connection refused
expense stays in queue when offline
expense stays in queue on DNS resolution failure
expense stays in queue on ERR_CONNECTION_RESET
first item sent, second fails — second stays in queue
all items sent on success — queue empty
failure at item 3 of 5 — items 3-5 remain in queue
AbortController fires after 30s — expense not lost
all fields are preserved exactly in IndexedDB
unicode characters in all fields preserved
rapid enqueue-flush cycles don't lose data
enqueue during failed flush preserves new item
enqueue rejection short-circuits the success side-effects
enqueue success runs the success side-effects exactly once
409 removes item from queue instead of retrying forever
flush assigns a client_expense_id when the queued row has none
explicit client_expense_id is preserved on enqueue and flush
drops a pre-v2 queued item (no category_id) instead of retrying it forever
remove only deletes the specified item
count returns zero for empty queue
each item gets a unique auto-increment id
remove with non-existent id does not throw
getAll returns items in insertion order
remove first item preserves remaining order
special characters in category and comment
first-paint flow: category dropdown has real options after applyDefault
Stale fallback — retries instead of sleeping until tomorrow
Drain runs on each tick when enabled
